mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for at least one month, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or greater signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal shows zero correlation with subsequent earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move in tandem above their 12-month trend, jointly signaling robust consumer and construction demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate profit growth accelerates across consumer discretionary and materials sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages simultaneously
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of co-trigger instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining above its 6-month low, indicating a temporary supply-side slowdown without structural collapse. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors interpret the slowdown as demand-constrained rather than capacity-driven.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month but remains above 6-month low
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling a transition from expansion to late-cycle labor market tightness. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes, as wage pressure remains contained and profit margins are defended.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100,000 and UNRATE unchanged month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal underperforms a simple PAYEMS-only signal by more than 0.05 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, indicating monetary policy is behind the inflation curve. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate imminent policy tightening and real rate compression.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter is positive and accelerating relative to the prior quarter, establishing a sustained demand expansion. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise with economic momentum.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when GDPC1 growth is positive and accelerating, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling a synchronized supply and labor contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors interpret the joint decline as recessionary pressure rather than temporary adjustment.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month in the same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of co-trigger instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over any 2-year rolling window.
